summary: Matched the common fighter DamageBind translation unit by deleting the old assembly file, switching ftCo_DamageBind.c from NonMatching to Matching in build configuration, and making small C/source-signature adjustments needed for codegen. The key source changes factored the damage calculation into a static helper, used explicit float constants for Fighter_ChangeMotionState and comparisons, and changed Player_GetHandicap from s8 to int with a u8 local so arithmetic/conversion behavior matched the original.
what_changed: Deleted asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageBind.s, removing the 199-line handwritten/disassembled assembly implementation for ftCo_DamageBind.
Marked melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageBind.c as Object(Matching, ...) in configure.py instead of Object(NonMatching, ...).
Changed obj_files.mk to build src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageBind.c.o instead of asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageBind.s.o.
Removed local forced-active literal definitions for ftCo_804D8D38, ftCo_804D8D40, and ftCo_804D8D44 from ftCo_DamageBind.c.
Introduced a static helper named commonCall(Fighter* fp) to isolate the ftCommon_8007DBCC damage/percent calculation.
Changed Fighter_ChangeMotionState arguments in ftCo_800C4550 from integer-looking constants 0, 1, 0 to explicit float constants 0.0f, 1.0f, 0.0f.
Changed the ftCo_DamageBind_Anim end condition comparison from fp->x1A4C <= 0 to fp->x1A4C <= 0.0f.
Changed Player_GetHandicap signature and declaration from s8 Player_GetHandicap(int slot) to int Player_GetHandicap(int slot), and changed its local handicap variable from s8 to u8.
smart_moves: Factoring the ftCommon_8007DBCC expression into commonCall appears to have been a codegen/matching tactic: it preserves the same arithmetic while allowing ftCo_800C4550 to match the original register/lifetime structure more closely.
Using explicit float literals for motion-state arguments and zero comparisons avoids unintended integer-to-float literal handling and better reflects the original lfs/floating-point code path.

Correcting Player_GetHandicap to return int with a u8 local is a cross-file matching fix: the DamageBind arithmetic depends on the handicap value being converted and used in floating-point calculations with the correct signedness/return-extension behavior.
The PR completed the standard decomp workflow by updating both configure.py and obj_files.mk when replacing an asm object with the matching C object.
decomp_lessons: Matching a fighter action file may require signature/type corrections in shared systems such as player.c/player.h, not just local changes in the fighter source file.
For PowerPC float-heavy code, writing constants as 0.0f and 1.0f can matter for matching even when the C semantics look equivalent to 0 and 1.
Signedness of small integer player fields can affect generated extension instructions and downstream integer-to-float conversion sequences; verify u8/s8/int choices against call-site assembly.
Extracting a repeated or complex expression into a static helper can be useful for matching register allocation and stack behavior, even if the helper is only used once in the final source.
Once a C file matches, remove the corresponding asm file and switch both object selection systems to the C object to prevent stale assembly from continuing to drive the build.
naming_conventions: Preserved existing ftCo symbol naming for unidentified common fighter functions: ftCo_800C44CC and ftCo_800C4550.
Action callback names use the ftCo_DamageBind_* pattern: ftCo_DamageBind_Anim, ftCo_DamageBind_IASA, ftCo_DamageBind_Phys, and ftCo_DamageBind_Coll.
The new local helper used lower camel case, commonCall, and was declared static because it is private to ftCo_DamageBind.c.
Player API names follow Player_Get*/Player_Set* naming, with the public declaration mirrored in src/melee/pl/player.h.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed sdata2 literal declarations from the C file after matching no longer required explicit forced-active local constants.
Used a helper function to shape the code around the ftCommon_8007DBCC call and its compound float expression.
Changed Player_GetHandicap return type from s8 to int and local storage from s8 to u8 to influence signedness/extension and conversion behavior.
Used explicit f32 literals in Fighter_ChangeMotionState and comparison code to align with original floating-point instructions.
Converted build inputs from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageBind.s.o to src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageBind.c.o after source matching.
follow_up_queries: Look for other player getter functions where s8/u8/int return signedness affects fighter matching.
Search ftCommon action files for complex ftCommon_8007DBCC expressions that may benefit from helper extraction for matching.
Audit remaining NonMatching ftCommon files adjacent to ftCo_DamageBind, such as ftCo_DamageSong, ftCo_0C35, ftCo_AirCatch, and ftCo_WarpStar.
Check whether commonCall should eventually receive a more descriptive name once ftCommon_8007DBCC and related damage bind formulas are understood.
